user_request: ubah sequence diagram jadi sesuai activity. Hanya perlu user (client/manager/teknisi), terus system, terus database. Drop "M M" pattern (Browser-T/Browser-M split). User left, system middle, right side either user or database — ngikut activity. User confirmed: align to 4 SD (drop REVIEW_GATE + ESKALASI, add UPLOAD_EVIDENCE). Dual-actor rule: User1|Sistem|DB|User2.

content:
Plan approved. Single specialist needed: Nakamura (Python tooling rewrite + file ops).

## Approved decisions
- Final SD count: 4 (align to activity count)
- Drop: SD_REVIEW_GATE.drawio, SD_ESKALASI.drawio
- Keep + restructure: SD_AUTENTIKASI, SD_PENGELOLAAN_PROYEK, SD_DASHBOARD_EWS
- Add new: SD_UPLOAD_EVIDENCE
- Lifeline rule single-actor: `User (Role) | Sistem | Database` (3 lifelines)
- Lifeline rule dual-actor (future): `User1 | Sistem | Database | User2` (4 lifelines)

## Final SD mapping
| File | Lifelines | Activity | Caption |
|------|-----------|----------|---------|
| SD_AUTENTIKASI | User \| Sistem \| Database | AD_LOGIN | Gambar 4.10 |
| SD_PENGELOLAAN_PROYEK | User (Manager) \| Sistem \| Database | AD_BUAT_PROYEK | Gambar 4.11 |
| SD_DASHBOARD_EWS | User (Manager) \| Sistem \| Database | AD_KELOLA_DASHBOARD | Gambar 4.12 |
| SD_UPLOAD_EVIDENCE | User (Teknisi) \| Sistem \| Database | AD_UPLOAD_EVIDENCE | Gambar 4.13 |

## Specialist plan
Nakamura solo — 5 sub-tasks all in his lane (Python rewrite, file delete, doc update):
1. Rewrite _gen.py spec functions (5 → 4, simplified lifelines, page width 1400 → ~700)
2. Run python _gen.py → emit 4 .drawio files
3. Delete obsolete SD_REVIEW_GATE.drawio + SD_ESKALASI.drawio
4. Update STANDARD.md Sequence section (lifeline rule)
5. Update TODO.md (5 → 4 SD, captions 4.10–4.13)

Plan file: C:\Users\GNERyze\.claude\plans\floofy-crunching-avalanche.md

No parallel needed (all sequential, single contributor scope).

content:
## Result

All 6 acceptance criteria met.

### Files changed
- `diagram/ai/Sequence/_gen.py` — full rewrite. 5 SD specs → 4. All lifelines collapsed to 3-column `User|Sistem|Database`. Page width 700. New `sd4_upload_evidence()` added.
- `diagram/STANDARD.md` — inserted "Lifeline rule (mirror activity layout)" subsection after lifeline header table (~line 142).
- `diagram/TODO.md` — SD count 5→4, captions 4.10-4.13, SD_REVIEW_GATE→SD_UPLOAD_EVIDENCE, SD_ESKALASI dropped, all checklist items marked done, lifeline tables updated.

### Generated (4 files, all XML-parseable)
- SD_AUTENTIKASI.drawio — 9262 bytes, Gambar 4.10
- SD_PENGELOLAAN_PROYEK.drawio — 13035 bytes, Gambar 4.11
- SD_DASHBOARD_EWS.drawio — 11765 bytes, Gambar 4.12
- SD_UPLOAD_EVIDENCE.drawio — 8547 bytes, Gambar 4.13

### Deleted (2 files)
- SD_REVIEW_GATE.drawio
- SD_ESKALASI.drawio

### Verification
- XML parse: all 4 clean
- Grep Browser-T|Browser-M|API Backend (Express): 0 matches
- ls shows exactly 4 SD_*.drawio + _gen.py

### SD4 flow design note
No AD_UPLOAD_EVIDENCE narrative in naskah/09_activity_diagram.md (that doc covers 5 old ADs). Flow derived from plan spec + existing task/evidence API patterns in _gen.py:
- GET /api/tasks/:id → render task detail
- POST /api/tasks/:id/evidence (multer multipart)
- PATCH tb_task status to working_on_it if was to_do
- alt fragment: valid file / invalid file (type/size check)
Tables: tb_task, tb_task_evidence

User feedback round 2: "harusnya itu lurus sequencenya, ga yg ada sub container kotak gitu, tolong cari diagram sequence yang sesuai akademis! bikin 3-7 life line sesuai activity aja! jangan nambah nambahin semau kamu!"

Then "uml" (typo'd "umr").

## Decisions confirmed
- **Style**: UML academic (linear, no combined fragments) — matches thesis author's own p50-p54
- **Lifeline count**: 3 per diagram (User|Sistem|Database) — user picked "Activity actors + Database always"
- **Fragments**: REMOVE ALL alt/loop/opt/par — user picked "Hapus semua, sequence lurus aja (happy path)"

## Naomi research findings
Source: thesis pages 50-54 + Indonesian academic refs (Dicoding, ITBox, Telkom Univ).
- Indonesian TA convention: linear flow, no fragments
- 3 lifelines typical (User|Sistem|Database)
- Header style: rectangles only (no actor stick figure in p50-54). KEEPING umlActor stick for User per current STANDARD — will note as discrepancy if user objects.

## Execution plan
Single Nakamura spawn:
1. Rewrite _gen.py spec functions: drop ALL `frag_begin/split/end` calls, convert conditional branches to linear happy-path
2. Regenerate 4 .drawio
3. Update STANDARD.md: note "academic style: omit combined fragments, prefer linear happy-path"
4. Verify grep: `alt \[|loop \[|opt \[|par \[` returns 0 matches

note: rewriting _gen.py: remove all fragments, linear happy-path only

content:
## Changes made

### _gen.py
- Removed `frag_begin()`, `frag_split()`, `frag_end()` methods entirely from SeqBuilder class
- Removed `fragment_cells()` primitive function
- Removed `frag_stack` state from `__init__`
- Rewrote all 4 spec functions as linear happy-path only:
  - sd1_autentikasi: dropped alt[valid/invalid], kept login happy path only (9 messages)
  - sd2_pengelolaan_proyek: dropped loop[setiap task], loop[setiap assignment], opt[notifikasi async] — kept single representative INSERT calls (20 messages)
  - sd3_dashboard_ews: dropped par[fetch chart data paralel] + loop[TanStack polling] — linearised 3 DB fetch calls sequentially (12 messages)
  - sd4_upload_evidence: dropped alt[file valid/invalid] — kept upload success path (11 messages)

### STANDARD.md
- Replaced Combined Fragment section (4 style lines) with academic-style OMIT notice + reference to p50-p54 thesis convention

## Output file sizes
| File | Bytes | Cells |
|---|---|---|
| SD_AUTENTIKASI.drawio | 6,302 | 19 |
| SD_PENGELOLAAN_PROYEK.drawio | 10,982 | 31 |
| SD_DASHBOARD_EWS.drawio | 8,096 | 23 |
| SD_UPLOAD_EVIDENCE.drawio | 7,220 | 21 |

Previous iteration (est): 35-62 cells with fragment boxes. Now 19-31 cells, all message-only.

## Verification
- Fragment grep (alt [|loop [|opt [|par [): 0 matches across all .drawio files
- XML parse: ALL OK (ET.parse on all 4 files)
- Dir listing: 4 SD_*.drawio + _gen.py confirmed
